text: {"claims": [{"statement": "High egg diets do not differ in weight loss compared to low egg diets in adults at risk of or with type 2 diabetes over three months.", "claimType": "empirical", "quote": "No significant differences in weight loss between high egg vs. low egg diets.", "entailment": "The quoted result directly reports no significant difference in weight loss between the two diet groups, supporting the claim.", "population": "adults at risk of T2D or with confirmed T2D", "intervention": "high egg diet", "comparator": "low egg diet", "outcome": "weight loss", "timeframe": "3 months", "quantifiers": "No significant differences", "modality": "causal"}, {"statement": "Egg breakfast leads to greater reported fullness than cereal breakfast in young adult females over a 7‑day energy‑restricted period.", "claimType": "empirical", "quote": "Reported fullness significantly greater after eggs.", "entailment": "The quote states that participants reported significantly greater fullness after eating eggs, directly supporting the claim.", "population": "60 females (24 ± 4.9 y)", "intervention": "egg breakfast", "comparator": "cereal breakfast", "outcome": "fullness", "timeframe": "7 d", "quantifiers": "significantly greater", "modality": "causal"}, {"statement": "Egg breakfast increases PYY levels three hours after consumption compared with bagel breakfast in children and adolescents.", "claimType": "empirical", "quote": "higher levels of PYY—an appetite-suppressing hormone—three hours after a breakfast with eggs compared with bagels.", "entailment": "The quoted observation directly reports higher PYY levels after an egg breakfast versus a bagel breakfast, supporting the claim.", "population": "children and adolescents", "intervention": "egg breakfast", "comparator": "bagel breakfast", "outcome": "PYY levels", "timeframe": "3 hours", "quantifiers": "higher levels", "modality": "causal"}, {"statement": "Egg breakfast reduces lunch energy intake compared with cereal or oatmeal breakfast.", "claimType": "empirical", "quote": "lower energy intake at lunch following an egg breakfast versus a cereal or oatmeal breakfast", "entailment": "The quote reports a lower lunch energy intake after an egg breakfast relative to cereal/oatmeal breakfasts, directly supporting the claim.", "population": "adults", "intervention": "egg breakfast", "comparator": "cereal or oatmeal breakfast", "outcome": "energy intake at lunch", "timeframe": "", "quantifiers": "lower", "modality": "causal"}, {"statement": "Egg breakfast leads to significantly lower total energy intake compared with an energy‑matched high carbohydrate breakfast.", "claimType": "empirical", "quote": "egg breakfast led to significantly lower energy intakes compared with an energy-matched high carbohydrate breakfast", "entailment": "The quoted result explicitly states a significantly lower energy intake for the egg breakfast versus the high‑carbohydrate breakfast.", "population": "adults", "intervention": "egg breakfast", "comparator": "energy-matched high carbohydrate breakfast", "outcome": "energy intake", "timeframe": "", "quantifiers": "significantly lower", boosting muscle protein synthesis", "entailment": "The quote directly claims that whole eggs are superior to egg whites for boosting muscle protein synthesis.", "population": "adults", "intervention": quote reports that both egg types produced significant beneficial changes in the listed outcomes.", "population": "adults", "intervention": "whole egg or egg white", "comparator": "", "outcome": "body metabolites compared with carbohydrate supplementation in young female athletes over eight weeks.", "claimType": "empirical", "quote": "only the egg group increased protein metabolites", "entailment": "The quote notes that the egg group, not the carbohydrate group, showed increased protein metabolites, supporting the claim.", "population": "young female athletes", "intervention": "egg supplementation", "comparator": "carbohydrate supplementation", "outcome": "protein metabolites", "timeframe": "8 weeks", "quantifiers": "increased", "modality": "causal"}, {"statement": "Animal
